metadata.title: Letter from Murray Sheehan to secretary to the president, Miami University, 1938
metadata.creator: Sheehan, Murray
subjects: Siamese Legation; College students; Universities and colleges; Correspondence
metadata.description: A letter from Murray Sheehan of the Siamese Legation to the secretary to the president of Miami University regarding the listing of Mr. Prasobsukh Sukhsvasti and the request to not grant him the title 'Prince'.
metadata.transcript:
[HANDWRITTEN] 1) RP
[HANDWRITTEN] 2) The M's - Pls note & return

STUDENTS' DEPARTMENT
MURRAY SHEEHAN, SUPERINTENDENT

2300 KALORAMA ROAD
WASHINGTON, D.C.

SIAMESE LEGATION

October 27, 1938

Secretary to the President
Miami University
Oxford, Ohio

My dear Miss Marshall:

Miss Aurelia Rosser has written to say that you would like to have Mr. Prasobsukh Sukhsvasti's present address and occupation for the 1938 graduate files.

Mr. Sukhsvasti should be listed as M. C. Prasobsukh Sukhsvasti, 10 Pra Aditya Road, Bangkok, Siam. I should like again, as on previous occasions, to request that the title of "Prince" be not granted to him by Miami University, despite Mr. Sukhsvasti's own apparent wide publicity on that score. His position in Siam may be, as before, a high-school teacher, or he may now be granted a position in one of the Government offices.

Unfortunately, Miss Rosser named you only as "Miss Marshall," so I am unable to address you more fully.

Yours very truly,

[HANDWRITTEN] Murray Sheehan

Superintendent
metadata.text_reading: RP. The M's - Please note and return. Students' Department. Murray Sheehan, Superintendent, 2300 Kalorama Road, Washington, D.C. Siamese Legation. October 27, 1938. His position in Siam may be, as before, a high-school teacher, or he may now be granted a position in one of the Government offices. Unfortunately, Miss Rosser named you only as 'Miss Marshall,' so I am unable to address you more fully. Yours very truly, Murray Sheehan, Superintendent.
